definitions.definition: To obtain; to acquire.

parsedExamples.example: I'm going to get a computer tomorrow from the discount store.
Lance is going to get Mary a ring.

examples: I'm going to get a computer tomorrow from the discount store.
Lance is going to get Mary a ring.

definitions.definition: To receive.

parsedExamples.example: I got a computer from my parents for my birthday.
He got a severe reprimand for that.

examples: I got a computer from my parents for my birthday.
He got a severe reprimand for that.

definitions.definition: To have. See usage notes.

parsedExamples.example: I've got a concert ticket for you.

examples: I've got a concert ticket for you.

definitions.definition: To fetch, bring, take.

parsedExamples.example: Can you get my bag from the living-room, please?
I need to get this to the office.

examples: Can you get my bag from the living-room, please?
I need to get this to the office.

definitions.definition: To become, or cause oneself to become .

parsedExamples.example: I'm getting hungry; how about you?
I'm going out to get drunk.

examples: I'm getting hungry; how about you?
I'm going out to get drunk.

definitions.definition: To cause to become; to bring about.

parsedExamples.example: That song gets me so depressed every time I hear it.
I'll get this finished by lunchtime.
I can't get these boots off.
I can’t get my hands warm.

examples: That song gets me so depressed every time I hear it.
I'll get this finished by lunchtime.
I can't get these boots off.
I can’t get my hands warm.

definitions.definition: To cause to do.

parsedExamples.example: Somehow she got him to agree to it.
I can't get it to work.
I can't get it working.
I can’t get my kids to go to bed early.

examples: Somehow she got him to agree to it.
I can't get it to work.
I can't get it working.
I can’t get my kids to go to bed early.

definitions.definition: To cause to come or go or move.

parsedExamples.example: I got him to his room.

examples: I got him to his room.

definitions.definition: To adopt, assume, arrive at, or progress towards (a certain position, location, state).

parsedExamples.example: The actors are getting into position.
When are we going to get to London?
I'm getting into a muddle.
We got behind the wall.

examples: The actors are getting into position.
When are we going to get to London?
I'm getting into a muddle.
We got behind the wall.

definitions.definition: To cover (a certain distance) while travelling.

parsedExamples.example: to get a mile

examples: to get a mile

definitions.definition: (with full infinitive or gerund-participle) To begin (doing something or to do something).

parsedExamples.example: We ought to get moving or we'll be late.
After lunch we got chatting.
I'm getting to like him better now.

examples: We ought to get moving or we'll be late.
After lunch we got chatting.
I'm getting to like him better now.

definitions.definition: To take or catch (a scheduled transportation service).

parsedExamples.example: I normally get the 7:45 train.
I'll get the 9 a.m. [flight] to Boston.

examples: I normally get the 7:45 train.
I'll get the 9 a.m. [flight] to Boston.

definitions.definition: To respond to (a telephone call, a doorbell, etc).

parsedExamples.example: Can you get that call, please? I'm busy.

examples: Can you get that call, please? I'm busy.

definitions.definition: (with full infinitive) To be able, be permitted, or have the opportunity (to do something desirable or ironically implied to be desirable).

parsedExamples.example: I'm so jealous that you got to see them perform live!
The finders get to keep 80 percent of the treasure.
Great. I get to clean the toilets today.

examples: I'm so jealous that you got to see them perform live!
The finders get to keep 80 percent of the treasure.
Great. I get to clean the toilets today.

definitions.definition: To understand.

parsedExamples.example: Yeah, I get it, it's just not funny.
I don't get what you mean by "fun". This place sucks!
I mentioned that I was feeling sad, so she mailed me a box of chocolates. She gets me.

examples: Yeah, I get it, it's just not funny.
I don't get what you mean by "fun". This place sucks!
I mentioned that I was feeling sad, so she mailed me a box of chocolates. She gets me.

definitions.definition: To be told; be the recipient of (a question, comparison, opinion, etc.).

parsedExamples.example: "You look just like Helen Mirren." / "I get that a lot."

examples: "You look just like Helen Mirren." / "I get that a lot."

definitions.definition: Used with the past participle to form the dynamic passive voice of a dynamic verb. Compared with static passive with to be, this emphasizes the commencement of an action or entry into a state.

parsedExamples.example: He got bitten by a dog.

examples: He got bitten by a dog.

definitions.definition: Used with a pronoun subject, usually you but sometimes one, to indicate that the object of the verb exists, can occur or is otherwise typical.

parsedExamples.example: You get some very rude people here.
It was the kind of shop you used to get in most small towns.

examples: You get some very rude people here.
It was the kind of shop you used to get in most small towns.

definitions.definition: To become ill with or catch (a disease).

parsedExamples.example: I went on holiday and got malaria.

examples: I went on holiday and got malaria.

definitions.definition: To catch out, trick successfully.

parsedExamples.example: He keeps calling pretending to be my boss—it gets me every time.

examples: He keeps calling pretending to be my boss—it gets me every time.

definitions.definition: To perplex, stump.

parsedExamples.example: That question's really got me.

examples: That question's really got me.

definitions.definition: To find as an answer.

parsedExamples.example: What did you get for question four?

examples: What did you get for question four?

definitions.definition: To bring to reckoning; to catch (usually as a criminal); to effect retribution.

parsedExamples.example: The cops finally got me.
I'm gonna get him for that.

examples: The cops finally got me.
I'm gonna get him for that.

definitions.definition: To hear completely; catch.

parsedExamples.example: Sorry, I didn't get that. Could you repeat it?

examples: Sorry, I didn't get that. Could you repeat it?

definitions.definition: To getter.

parsedExamples.example: I put the getter into the container to get the gases.

examples: I put the getter into the container to get the gases.

definitions.definition: To beget (of a father).
To learn; to commit to memory; to memorize; sometimes with out.

parsedExamples.example: to get a lesson;  to get out one's Greek lesson

examples: to get a lesson;  to get out one's Greek lesson

definitions.definition: Used with a personal pronoun to indicate that someone is being pretentious or grandiose.

parsedExamples.example: Get her with her new hairdo.

examples: Get her with her new hairdo.

definitions.definition: To go, to leave; to scram.
To kill.

parsedExamples.example: They’re coming to get you, Barbara.

examples: They’re coming to get you, Barbara.

definitions.definition: To make acquisitions; to gain; to profit.
To measure.

parsedExamples.example: Did you get her temperature?

examples: Did you get her temperature?

definitions.definition: To cause someone to laugh.

parsedExamples.example: It gets me every time!

examples: It gets me every time!

definitions.definition: Offspring, especially illegitimate.
Lineage.
A difficult return or block of a shot.
Something gotten, something gained or won; an acquisition.
A message or post on an online platform, particularly imageboards, with a unique identifier deemed special or rare, usually due to patterns in the ID.
Synonym of git (“contemptible person”).
A Jewish writ of divorce.
rotten
Medial form of gete
jet, hardened coal
A bead made of jet.
A jet-black pigment.
plural of got (“goat”)
alternative form of ġeat
alternative form of ġīet
a guess
first-person singular present indicative of geta
second-person singular imperative of geta
goat
to get
second-person singular imperative of getmək
to understand

parsedExamples.translation: keoi gong ge je taai fuk zaap, ngo get m dou keoi me ji si. [Jyutping]
parsedExamples.example: <link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Cantonese_terms_with_usage_examples" about="#mwt902">

examples: <link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Cantonese_terms_with_usage_examples" about="#mwt902">

definitions.definition:
inflection of geta:
  1. first-person singular present indicative
  2. singular imperative
first-person singular present indicative
singular imperative
divorce
some, somewhat
very

parsedExamples.example: Ich woar mer get blij.

examples: Ich woar mer get blij.

definitions.definition: something, anything
Placed before a plural noun, indicating general cases of people or things: some

parsedExamples.example: Doe has get höng.

examples: Doe has get höng.

definitions.definition: Get, one of the Getae, Greek name for the Dacian people
goat
